purpose: Coach-only internal calibration prompts. Coach answers should be treated as evidence signals, not objective ratings, and must not be presented as teacher-facing scores or progress labels.

calibration_questions.question: What best describes the teacher's recent implementation?

options.label: Sustained implementation
options.description: Used the strategy repeatedly over time.

options.label: One-time attempt
options.description: Tried it once or briefly.

options.label: Planned but not attempted
options.description: Discussed or planned the move but has not yet tried it.

options.label: Reverted to old habits
options.description: Initially tried the move but returned to prior practice.

options.label: Unknown
options.description: There is not enough evidence yet.

calibration_questions.question: After trying the strategy, what happened next?

options.label: Adapted based on evidence
options.description: Adjusted the approach based on what happened in class.

options.label: Repeated without adjustment
options.description: Repeated the same move without much reflection or adaptation.

options.label: Stopped after difficulty
options.description: Stopped when the strategy felt awkward, hard, or unclear.

options.label: Waiting for more direction
options.description: Appears to need the coach to prescribe the next step.

options.label: Unknown
options.description: There is not enough evidence yet.

calibration_questions.question: How did the teacher talk about the work?

options.label: Owns decisions and learning
options.description: Names what they tried, why, and what they learned.

options.label: Partial ownership
options.description: Acknowledges some role but focuses heavily on constraints.

options.label: Externalises responsibility
options.description: Attributes lack of progress mostly to students, context, time, or materials.

options.label: Defensive or dismissive
options.description: Resists examining their own practice or dismisses the coaching focus.

options.label: Unknown
options.description: There is not enough evidence yet.

calibration_questions.question: Did the teacher take an instructional risk?

options.label: Meaningful risk taken
options.description: Tried something outside normal comfort zone.

options.label: Small safe adjustment
options.description: Made a minor low-risk change.

options.label: Avoided risk
options.description: Chose not to try a needed stretch move.

options.label: Risk not relevant
options.description: This session does not provide evidence about risk-taking.

options.label: Unknown
options.description: There is not enough evidence yet.

options.label: Teacher artifact
options.description: Lesson plan, revised materials, reflection, or unit plan shows the work.

options.label: Student artifact
options.description: Student work, exit tickets, discussion evidence, or assessment responses show the impact.

options.label: Observation evidence
options.description: Coach or peer directly saw the implementation.

options.label: Anecdotal only
options.description: The coach heard a description but has not seen artifacts or implementation evidence.

options.label: None or unknown
options.description: No concrete evidence is currently available.

options.label: Positive student shift
options.description: Engagement, participation, work quality, or understanding appears to have improved.

options.label: Mixed or unclear shift
options.description: Some signs are visible, but not consistently enough to infer progress.

options.label: No observed shift
options.description: No meaningful student-facing change has been observed yet.

options.label: Negative or resistant shift
options.description: Student response worsened or resistance increased.

options.label: Not observed
options.description: Student-facing evidence has not yet been examined.

calibration_questions.question: During coaching, the teacher was mostly:

options.label: Active problem-solver
options.description: Asked questions, tested ideas, reflected, and helped shape the next step.

options.label: Compliant participant
options.description: Went through the steps but showed little initiative or ownership.

options.label: Passive observer
options.description: Listened or watched but did not engage deeply with practice.

options.label: Defensive participant
options.description: Protected current practice or rejected feedback.

options.label: Unknown
options.description: There is not enough evidence yet.

calibration_questions.question: If resistance appeared, what best explains it?

options.label: Productive discomfort
options.description: The discomfort seems connected to a meaningful professional stretch.

options.label: Coach-induced resistance
options.description: A coaching move may have created defensiveness, mistrust, or loss of agency.

options.label: Change avoidance
options.description: Teacher avoided a needed change despite support.

options.label: System constraint
options.description: Barrier appears mostly structural, time-based, or resource-based.

options.label: No resistance observed
options.description: Resistance was not visible in this session.

options.label: Unknown
options.description: There is not enough evidence yet.

calibration_questions.question: Which classroom talk pattern is most visible?

options.label: Group shouting / few students
options.description: Students often shout together or a few students dominate while others stay quiet.

options.label: Early individual volunteers
options.description: Some group shouting remains, but the teacher is beginning to invite individual responses.

options.label: Targeted questioning and pauses
options.description: Teacher targets individuals or volunteers and uses pauses for thinking time.

options.label: Multiple participation modes
options.description: Teacher uses mini-whiteboards, pair talk, small groups, or other participation structures.

options.label: Purposeful movement between talk modes
options.description: Teacher chooses talk structures based on lesson goal and uses group ideas in whole-class discussion.

options.label: Unknown
options.description: There is not enough evidence yet.

calibration_questions.question: Which reflection pattern best fits the teacher's current reflection?

options.label: Action-focused
options.description: Focuses mainly on what was done, with little attention to assumptions or framing.

options.label: Surface cause / single-loop
options.description: Names surface causes or external factors and proposes tweaks within the same frame.

options.label: Assumption-aware
options.description: Identifies assumptions and can consider alternative frames with prompting.

options.label: Reframing and transfer
options.description: Questions governing beliefs, reconstructs the problem, and generalises learning.

options.label: Unknown
options.description: There is not enough evidence yet.

levels.label: Group shouting / limited targeting
levels.description: Students often shout answers together. Questions are mostly not aimed at specific students. A few students dominate while others stay quiet or copy.
levels.coaching_implication: Support the coach to begin with participation structures and protected thinking time.

levels.label: Early individual participation
levels.description: Some group shouting remains, but the teacher starts asking individuals to volunteer and notices quiet students.
levels.coaching_implication: Support routines for inviting individuals without increasing anxiety.

levels.label: Targeted questioning and pauses
levels.description: Group shouting largely stops. The teacher asks volunteers or specific students and uses pauses to give thinking time.
levels.coaching_implication: Build from participation into uptake, probing, and pair rehearsal.

levels.label: Multiple participation modes
levels.description: Teacher uses varied ways to ask questions, including individuals, mini-whiteboards, pairs, or small groups.
levels.coaching_implication: Support diagnostic selection of talk structures and stronger student-to-student talk.

levels.label: Purposeful movement between talk modes
levels.description: Teacher chooses questioning mode based on prior knowledge and lesson goal, then uses small-group ideas to lead whole-class discussion.
levels.coaching_implication: Support refinement, transfer, and teacher artistry rather than basic participation routines.

levels.label: Action-focused
levels.description: Reflection focuses on what was done with little awareness of assumptions, framing, or governing beliefs.
levels.coaching_implication: Use gentle questions that connect actions to beliefs and perceived risks.

levels.label: Surface cause / single-loop
levels.description: Reflection names surface reasons or external factors and tends to propose tweaks within the same frame.
levels.coaching_implication: Invite the teacher to compare interpretations and test whether the original framing is helping.

levels.label: Assumption-aware
levels.description: Reflection identifies main assumptions and can consider alternative ways to frame the situation with prompting.
levels.coaching_implication: Use moderate challenge to connect reframing to a new classroom experiment.

levels.label: Reframing and transfer
levels.description: Reflection explicitly questions governing beliefs, reconstructs the problem, and generalises learning to future contexts.
levels.coaching_implication: Use direct, expansive questions about transfer, principles, and adaptive expertise.

levels.label: Rigid
levels.description: Teacher tends to continue the original plan even when students show confusion or disengagement.
levels.coaching_implication: Plan one in-the-moment cue that signals when to pause or adjust.

levels.label: Reactive
levels.description: Teacher notices difficulty but adapts after hesitation or mainly through standard procedures.
levels.coaching_implication: Support a small decision tree for likely barriers.

levels.label: Adaptive artistry
levels.description: Teacher notices unexpected variables and tests revised actions in the moment without losing learning focus.
levels.coaching_implication: Help name the tacit judgement so it can transfer to new contexts.

levels.label: Surface description
levels.description: Teacher describes events but does not evaluate causes or assumptions; may mainly attribute difficulty externally.

levels.label: Descriptive improvement
levels.description: Teacher accurately states what happened and offers a broad improvement idea, but root causes remain underexplored.
levels.coaching_implication: Move from description to why this pattern occurred.

levels.label: Root-cause reframing
levels.description: Teacher identifies why outcomes differed from expectations and converts insight into future action.
levels.coaching_implication: Support transfer and precise implementation planning.

levels.label: Low openness to feedback
levels.description: Teacher may reject feedback, avoid introspection, or assume no further refinement is needed.
levels.coaching_implication: Use autonomy-supportive language and small safe experiments.

levels.label: Episodic reflection
levels.description: Teacher reflects when prompted but learning is not yet a continuous improvement cycle.
levels.coaching_implication: Connect one reflection routine to a repeated classroom practice.

levels.label: Growth-oriented artistry
levels.description: Teacher shows evolving thinking and enthusiasm for refining practice over time.
levels.coaching_implication: Use more ambitious inquiry and transfer questions.
